summary: Iteration 5 frontend testing — new Meters tab + pro-fader CSS + regressions. All critical NEW-feature flows PASSED: TopBar exposes 3 tabs (tab-channels, tab-meters, tab-matrix); clicking tab-meters mounts meters-view with header 'All Channels · Meters' and two sections (Inputs / Outputs). Per-channel meter columns and dB readouts are present for every channel using the documented testid pattern (meter-{id} + meter-{id}-db). v16 default renders 16 phy + 16 virt for both inputs and outputs (64 meter columns, 64 db readouts). Switching to v8 via version-confirm-modal correctly re-renders 8 phy + 8 virt each side (32 total), and the section header text reads '· 16 ch' for each side as expected (8+8). All regression flows verified: tab cycling produces zero pageerror events, EQ modal (sel-open-eq-modal → eq-modal), COMP modal (sel-open-comp-modal → comp-modal), sel-mute toggling, channel pills (pill-out_phy_0 selectable), routing matrix (route-* cells + matrix-clear-all rendered), preset modal (preset-modal opens), Pink Noise master toggle flips state to 'ALL ON'. .vertical-fader CSS class is applied to both sel-fader (SelectedChannelPanel.jsx:290) and channel-strip out-{kind}-{idx}-fader (ChannelStrip.jsx:277) per source review.

integration_issues.issue: Could not functionally verify output meters showing pink-noise activity in headless Chromium. After clicking pn-master-toggle (ALL ON) all output dB readouts remained '−∞' and 0 segments lit. Cause: AudioContext stays suspended in headless mode (35x 'AudioContext was not allowed to start' warnings logged). The full audio graph is wired correctly per code review (audioEngine.js pinkSrc→pinkGain→input bus, analyser downstream) and pn-master-toggle does call ensureContext().resume(). This is a TEST ENVIRONMENT limitation rather than an app bug — manual verification in a real browser tab is required to confirm meter movement. The state propagation itself was verified (toggle text flipped to 'ALL ON').

issues: Screenshot confirms clean pro-audio layout: dBFS scale column on left of each section, color legend (green/yellow/red) top-right, 32 ch label per section, channel names + PHY/VIRT subtitle + 32-segment vertical meter + dB peak readout per column. No overlap or contrast problems.
Chromium 'slider-vertical appearance deprecated' warning still emitted by .vertical-fader CSS — purely a console warning, no functional impact (noted in iteration 3/4).
AudioContext autoplay warnings on initial load — expected per spec, requires user gesture to resume; not a regression.

action_items: (Optional, manual) Verify in a real browser tab that with PN ALL ON the output meter strips in MetersView visibly fill (green/yellow/red segments) — automated headless test could not confirm because Chromium's AudioContext stays suspended.

(Carry-over cosmetic) Consider replacing deprecated `appearance: slider-vertical` with the standardized `writing-mode: vertical-lr; direction: rtl` approach to silence the Chromium deprecation warning.
critical_code_review_comments: MetersView.jsx is well-structured (~165 lines, under the 700-line guideline): MeterColumn/MeterPeakDb are extracted as sub-components so each meter has its own RAF loop, preventing parent rerenders.

MeterPeakDb peak-decay logic (line 58) decays peak by *0.95 once per RAF after a 1.5 s hold — this decays roughly 60×/sec which is fast; consider rate-limiting decay to once per 50 ms for a more 'pro' peak-hold feel. Cosmetic only.
Meter.jsx now branches on source ∈ {'in','out','inputBus'} (line 32) — clean addition; matches audioEngine.getInputBusLevel signature.
audioEngine.js correctly creates `inputBusAnalysers[inp.id]` per input bus (line 75) and tears them down in teardownGraph (line 218). No leak.
TopBar.jsx tab array now hard-codes ids in source (line 51–55). Order is channels → meters → matrix, matching spec. tabs are rendered with proper data-testid and active highlight.
DSPApp.jsx renders MetersView when tab==='meters' — confirmed by data-testid='meters-view' appearing on tab switch.
SectionHeader uses `outputs.length + virtual.length` for the channel count — correct for both v16 (16+16=32) and v8 (8+8=16) variants.

MetersView renders 16 sections worth of children in a single non-virtualized flex row; for v16 that is 64 columns each running its own RAF loop (Meter + MeterPeakDb) = 128 RAF loops. Acceptable on desktop but could be expensive on low-power devices; consider a single shared RAF tick that broadcasts to subscribers if performance regressions appear.
.vertical-fader CSS class applied to both sel-fader (SelectedChannelPanel.jsx:290) and ChannelStrip OUT fader (ChannelStrip.jsx:277) — confirmed via grep.

Color legend in MetersView header (≤-6 / -6 to -2 / >-2) does not 100 % match the Meter.jsx segment thresholds (`i >= segments-2 → red`, `i >= segments-5 → yellow`) which translate roughly to top 6 % red / top 16 % yellow. With 32 segments mapped to ~-60..0 dB (linear segment scale), the red segments correspond to roughly the top ~4 dB not the top -2 dB shown in the legend. Minor labeling inconsistency.
MeterPeakDb dbFromLevel uses `20*log10(level)` on a 0..1 amplitude — correct for RMS-style amplitude, matching engine analyser output range.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of UI/integration flows that can be verified headlessly; pink-noise → meter movement requires manual visual check

test_credentials: n/a — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: None — localStorage cleared before each test, used default state
context_for_next_testing_agent: MetersView testids: meters-view (root), meter-{channel.id} per column (e.g. meter-in_phy_0, meter-in_virt_15, meter-out_phy_0, meter-out_virt_15), meter-{channel.id}-db for peak readout. Tabs: tab-channels, tab-meters, tab-matrix. NOTE — actual default DSP version is v16 (NOT v32 as stated in the review_request) → 16 phy + 16 virt each side = 64 meter columns total. v8 → 8+8 each = 32 columns. Section header text uses '· {n} ch' format. PN master testid: pn-master-toggle. AudioContext requires user-gesture-driven resume; in headless Chromium meters will read −∞ even with PN ON — verify visually in a real browser. ChannelStrip output gain fader testid pattern: out-{kind}-{idx}-fader and out-{kind}-{idx}-gain-value where kind ∈ {out_phy,out_virt}. SelectedChannelPanel uses sel-open-eq-modal / sel-open-comp-modal (NOT sel-eq-open) and sel-delay-value (NOT sel-delay). Preset modal root is data-testid='preset-modal' (NOT preset-manager). When testing React-controlled <input type=range>, set value via the native HTMLInputElement value setter prototype descriptor and dispatch BOTH 'input' and 'change' events — but be aware that some sliders only commit on 'change'.
rca of the issue: No application bugs identified. The only unverified flow (pink-noise → output meter activity) is blocked by Chromium headless audio policy, not by code: the audio graph is correctly wired (audioEngine creates pinkSrc on each output chain, gates via pinkGain, runs into the chain analyser used by getOutputLevel) and the master toggle's click handler calls ensureContext().resume(). State propagation (pinkAllOn → 'ALL ON' label, per-channel toggle) was verified. Suggest manual visual confirmation in a real browser to close the loop. All other new-feature requirements (tabs, MetersView layout, per-channel meters/dB, v8/v16 channel counts) are functionally correct and visually polished per the captured screenshot.
